commit.message:
fix(seo): add expired data for Artisa Group job to fix canonical→listing

The job 'segretaria-assistente-progetto-artisa-group-lugano' was removed
from active data but never archived to expired, causing its soft-landing
page to have minimal content. Google's canonicalization then chose the
listing page instead of the self-referencing canonical.

This adds proper expired entries with translated titles, descriptions,
and salary data so the build plugin generates enriched soft-landing pages
that Google will respect as canonical.

Root cause: the Artisa crawler removed the job from its slice but the
daily cleanup-jobs.mjs had not yet run to archive it. The compat-paths
pipeline still generated a page, but without expired metadata the content
was too thin for Google to treat as a distinct page.

commit.message:
fix(eoc-crawler): prevent slug churn from location instability

EOC jobs accumulated 10+ previousSlugs each (958 total), bloating the
build with thousands of redirect pages. Root causes:

1. Location instability: extractEocLocation() produces different cities
   across crawl runs (Bellinzona→Novaggio→Lugano) depending on which
   hospital name appears first in the description. The base crawler's
   merge step treats location changes as genuine slug changes.

2. No cap on previousSlugs: every slug change accumulated indefinitely.

Fixes:
- Add stabilizeEocSlugsInMemory(): compares post-merge jobs with
  pre-crawl slice data by vacancy ID. If the title is unchanged, any
  slug change is location-driven and gets reverted.
- Add previousSlugs capping (MAX_PREVIOUS_SLUGS=5) in
  postProcessEocJobsInMemory(): deduplicates against current slugs,
  then keeps only the 5 most recent entries.
- Cap existing data: 958→660 previousSlugs (31% reduction).

Tests: 12 tests pass (5 new covering stabilization + capping).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME directly instead of dirs::config_dir() (#284)

* Initial plan

* Fix XDG directory table in docs to match dirs v6 behavior

The documentation table showed stale macOS and Windows paths from
dirs v5. With dirs v6:
- macOS config_dir() returns ~/Library/Application Support (not
  ~/Library/Preferences)
- Windows config_dir() returns %APPDATA% (not %LOCALAPPDATA%)
- state_dir() returns None on macOS and Windows (falls back to
  data directory)

This made the config file appear to be in $XDG_STATE_HOME territory
on macOS, when it was actually in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME all along.

Fixes #283

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME directly instead of dirs::config_dir()

Replace all 4 uses of `dirs::config_dir()` with a new `xdg_config_home()`
helper that reads `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (falling back to `$HOME/.config`).
This follows the XDG Base Directory spec directly rather than using the
platform-specific mappings from the `dirs` crate, which on macOS returns
`~/Library/Application Support` instead of `~/.config`.

The documentation table in `docs/configuration.md` is simplified to show
only the XDG defaults since the paths are now consistent across platforms.

Fixes #283

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: improve xdg_config_home docs and test names

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use %APPDATA% as config fallback on Windows instead of ~/.config

On Windows, fall back to %APPDATA% (the conventional roaming config
directory) when $XDG_CONFIG_HOME is not set. On Unix/macOS the fallback
remains $HOME/.config. The XDG env var is always respected on every
platform when explicitly set.

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract shared home_dot_config() helper and improve Windows test

Address code review: deduplicate the $HOME/.config fallback into a shared
helper function, and use expect() in the Windows test so it fails
explicitly when %APPDATA% is unexpectedly missing.

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: return Option from xdg_config_home, validate XDG values, parameterized tests

- xdg_config_home() now returns Option<PathBuf> (None when no config dir
  can be determined), fixing the relative-path fallback issue
- Ignores empty and non-absolute $XDG_CONFIG_HOME values per XDG spec
- Extracted pure resolve_config_home() helper for deterministic testing
- Updated all 4 call sites to propagate the Option properly
- config_path_from/components_path_from now return Option<PathBuf>
- Store::open and StateInfo::new use .context() for clear error messages
- 8 deterministic unit tests covering all resolve_config_home branches

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix spelling: Honour -> Honor for codebase consistency

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Windows CI: use platform-appropriate absolute paths in xdg tests

Unix-style paths like /custom/config are not absolute on Windows
(they lack a drive letter). Add an abs() test helper that prefixes
C: on Windows so all resolve_config_home tests work cross-platform.

commit.message:
fix: wrap Schindler slice in proper {crawlerKey, assembledAt, jobs} format

The Schindler data file was a bare JSON array instead of the wrapper
object expected by assemble-jobs-dataset.mjs. The assembly script
checks for `Array.isArray(slice.jobs)` and skips slices that don't
match, causing Schindler to be reported as 'malformed' during deploy.

Root cause: the file predates the migration to per-crawler slice format.
The crawler's current code correctly calls writeJobsCrawlerSlice() which
produces the proper wrapper, but the stale file was never re-crawled to
update its structure.

commit.message:
consensus: fix finality-lock busy-loop with proper cooldown field

- Add pub fork_resolution_blocked_until: Arc<AtomicU64> to Blockchain
  struct (Unix-second deadline). Replaces the previous attempt that
  wrote same_height_fork_cooldown with our own hash instead of the
  peer's hash (so it never matched the check in compare_chain_with_peers
  and the cooldown had no effect).

- Set fork_resolution_blocked_until = now+120s whenever the finality
  lock fires and blocks a reorg.  This prevents the full deep-fetch
  cycle from being restarted every ~15 s (one ping interval) before
  the 60 s longer_chain_escape timer can accumulate.

- Guard handle_fork() entry: if cooldown is active and the call is
  NOT a continuation of an already-active FetchingChain for this peer,
  return early with a debug log.

- Extend the ping-handler GetBlocks guard in message_handler.rs to
  also check fork_resolution_blocked_until, so the cooldown is
  respected from both spawn paths (handle_fork + ping handler).

- Also added in prior edits (same session):
    * MIN_PEERS_FINALITY_OVERRIDE: 5 → 2
    * longer_chain_escape: bypass finality lock after 60s if even 1
      peer is strictly ahead and majority of ahead-peers agree
    * ping handler fork_state guard (ForkResolutionState != None)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
